redacted: The live 'Account:' row carried the recording operator's ChatGPT login. It is replaced byte-for-byte with 'user@example.com' (16 chars in, 16 chars out), so every column of the box renders exactly as captured. This is the ONLY edit to bytes.raw.
notes: Hand-recorded probe pinning the 'bypass' rung: -s danger-full-access -a never renders 'Full Access', confirming the table row. Note the asymmetry this recording proves: with the danger-full-access sandbox the approval policy vanishes from the rendering entirely (no parenthetical), so -a never is invisible here while it IS load-bearing under workspace-write (see status-auto). Widths are measured from the rendered 120x40 screen. widest_status_row_cols/status_box_outer_cols is the box's rendered width; intrinsic_row_cols is the minimum unwrapped width each row needs (opening bar + label column + value + closing bar), which is what CODEX_STATUS_MIN_COLS has to clear. NOTE the Session: row needs 62 cols, above the current CODEX_STATUS_MIN_COLS = 60 — a documented FOLLOW-UP to raise the constant with this evidence, not a defect here: both the session scrape and the permission parse anchor the closing bar on the same physical line, so a wrapped row fails closed at any width.
